evidence: Live schema requires `kolm_yaml_version`, `name`, and `artifacts`, and forbids additional properties.
code_truth: `parseProjectYaml` defaults missing fields and ignores unknown fields without validation.
risk: A file can appear schema-invalid yet still drive runtime behavior.
recommended_action: Add schema validation and make CLI/MCP use the validated result.

evidence: `src/project.js` says it hand-parses only the subset read at runtime.
code_truth: The parser does not implement full JSON-schema/YAML behavior and silently skips malformed lines.
risk: Docs can promise config shapes that runtime ignores.
recommended_action: Document the supported subset or adopt a real YAML parser plus schema validator.

evidence: Schema hook arrays accept string items only.
code_truth: `src/hooks.js` supports strings, inline lists, and flow objects with `command` and `timeout_ms`; `docs/HOOKS.md` documents objects.
risk: Valid documented hooks can fail schema validation.
recommended_action: Update schema to include hook object forms or remove object docs.

evidence: Live schema says stdout JSON may modify the event with `additionalContext` or `updatedInput`.
code_truth: Runtime captures stdout but never parses or applies event mutations.
risk: Policy and redaction hooks cannot actually rewrite inputs despite schema wording.
recommended_action: Implement a tested mutation schema or delete the claim.

evidence: Docs describe hooks as policy, telemetry, registry push, and safety interposition.
code_truth: Runtime executes hook strings through `cmd /c` or `/bin/sh -c` with project root as cwd.
risk: Project files become local arbitrary command execution surfaces.
recommended_action: Add trust warnings, `doctor --hooks`, and explicit review/dry-run output before applying untrusted project configs.

evidence: Docs say exit code 2 blocks and other non-zero codes warn.
code_truth: Runtime blocks only on exit code 2; all other failures continue.
risk: Policy hooks can fail open unless authors know to exit exactly 2.
recommended_action: Provide blocking/advisory modes and make Pre* policy hooks fail closed by default.

evidence: Hooks have timeout support and default to 30 seconds.
code_truth: Timeouts finish as exit code 124 and therefore continue as advisory failures.
risk: A hung or timed-out policy hook can let compile/run/bench proceed.
recommended_action: Treat timeouts as blocking for Pre* hooks or require explicit advisory mode.

evidence: `KOLM_HOOKS_OFF=1` disables all hooks.
code_truth: Bypass returns skipped with no persistent audit row.
risk: A local environment variable can bypass policy without traceability.
recommended_action: Log hook bypasses and make doctor display the bypass state.

evidence: CLI prints hook result tails and stores stdout/stderr in memory.
code_truth: Outputs are truncated to 4000 chars and not written to a durable project audit log.
risk: Policy evidence can be lost and stderr can leak secrets into terminal logs.
recommended_action: Add structured hook audit rows with redaction and retention policy.

evidence: Schema defines arrays for `allowed-tools`, `paths`, and hooks.
code_truth: Runtime only supports simple inline list parsing for project fields and a limited block parser for hooks.
risk: Schema-valid block arrays can be ignored by runtime.
recommended_action: Add parser tests for schema examples and reject unsupported forms loudly.

evidence: Live build-your-own says path globs auto-attach artifacts when agents edit matching files.
code_truth: Runtime parses `paths` and includes them in private MCP metadata, but no harness auto-attach logic was found.
risk: Users can expect agent routing behavior that is not implemented.
recommended_action: Mark paths as metadata only or implement harness-specific auto-attach tests.

evidence: Schema and sidecar copy describe `allowed-tools` as the tools a sub-agent may call.
code_truth: Project parser stores `allowed_tools`, but MCP strips metadata from wire output and generated sidecars hardcode `allowed-tools: []`.
risk: The config implies tool permissions that are not enforced.
recommended_action: Either enforce allowed tools in harness sidecars/MCP or remove the governance claim.

evidence: Schema caps `k_min` at 1 and generated comments show `k_min: 0.85`.
code_truth: Current fixture artifacts report K-score composites in the hundreds; MCP compares raw composite to `k_min`.
risk: The default-looking floor does not filter current artifacts.
recommended_action: Normalize K-score or migrate manifests before using `k_min` as a gate.

evidence: Schema allows `mcp.transport: sse` and generated comments mention an SSE port.
code_truth: CLI serve implements `--mcp` with optional `--http`; MCP server implements stdio and HTTP JSON-RPC only.
risk: Config can declare a transport that cannot run.
recommended_action: Remove SSE from schema/comments until implemented and tested.

evidence: Schema defines `mcp.transport`, `host`, and `port`.
code_truth: `cmdServe` reads CLI flags for `--http` and `--port`; it does not apply project `mcp` config.
risk: `kolm.yaml` can appear to configure serve behavior while being ignored.
recommended_action: Make serve load project mcp config or document flags as the only active controls.

evidence: Live build-your-own says `kolm doctor` verifies wiring.
code_truth: `cmdDoctor` checks config, API key, cloud health, receipt secret, Node, Docker, optional Anthropic key, project config, and artifact counts.
risk: Doctor does not smoke installed harness configs, MCP initialize/list, hooks, sidecars, or `k_min`.
recommended_action: Add harness/MCP/hook checks or narrow the wording.

evidence: Live docs snippet says `kolm init` scaffolds `kolm.yaml` plus examples.
code_truth: `cmdInit` writes `kolm.yaml`, creates `.kolm/artifacts` and `.kolm/skills`, and updates `.gitignore`; it does not create examples.
risk: First-run docs can overpromise generated files.
recommended_action: Fix docs or add example scaffolding.

evidence: `cmdInit` creates `.kolm/artifacts` and `.kolm/skills`.
code_truth: Implementation matches build-your-own page output for the local working dirs.
risk: This part of project bootstrap is reliable.
recommended_action: Keep and add an init smoke test to freeze behavior.

evidence: Build-your-own page shows `.gitignore` updated with `.kolm/`.
code_truth: `cmdInit` appends `.kolm/` when a git repo or `.gitignore` exists and avoids ignoring `kolm.yaml`.
risk: Good local artifact hygiene, but untested.
recommended_action: Add a non-destructive init fixture test.

evidence: Build-your-own says every compile emits a SKILL.md sidecar next to the artifact or under `skills_dir`.
code_truth: Compile paths call `writeSkillSidecar` unless `--no-skill` is passed.
risk: Sidecar emission is real but the sidecar content has drift.
recommended_action: Keep behavior, but generate from validated project config.

evidence: Generated sidecar says runtime egress is patched at the process boundary.
code_truth: Egress monitor is implemented in benchmark code, not in `kolm run` or MCP calls.
risk: Generated proof text can overstate run-time isolation.
recommended_action: Rewrite sidecar guarantee or move egress monitor into runtime.

evidence: Search of `tests/` found no focused hook or project parser tests.
code_truth: Root suite does not cover `parseProjectYaml`, `parseHooksBlock`, `runHooks`, `KOLM_HOOKS_OFF`, or hook blocking.
risk: Parser and policy drift can ship silently.
recommended_action: Add unit tests with temp project dirs and fake hook scripts.

evidence: `node --check` passes for `services/mcp/server.js`.
code_truth: The service file is syntactically valid.
risk: Syntax health does not prove transport or config behavior.
recommended_action: Add protocol smoke tests for stdio and HTTP with project config.

evidence: `node --check` passes for `src/hooks.js` and `src/project.js`.
code_truth: Core config files are syntactically valid.
risk: Semantic drift remains untested.
recommended_action: Pair syntax checks with parser fixtures.

evidence: CLI has `findProjectKolmYaml` for sidecars while `src/project.js` has the main project parser.
code_truth: Sidecar generation only reads `name` and `skills_dir`, not artifact metadata or allowed tools.
risk: Two parsers can drift and produce different project behavior.
recommended_action: Use `src/project.js` everywhere.

evidence: Hooks and project config walk up from cwd looking for `kolm.yaml`.
code_truth: A command inside a nested directory can execute hooks from an ancestor project.
risk: This is convenient but risky in untrusted checkouts.
recommended_action: Display discovered project root before running hooks and add a `--no-hooks` CLI flag.

evidence: Doctor does not list which hook commands would run.
code_truth: Users cannot easily audit hook side effects before compile/run/bench.
risk: Untrusted project configs can surprise users.
recommended_action: Add `kolm doctor --hooks` or `kolm hooks list`.

evidence: Live `kolm-yaml-v0.1.json` returns HTTP 200.
code_truth: The public schema is reachable and parseable.
risk: Availability is good, but contract drift remains.
recommended_action: Keep the schema URL and make it the actual validation source.

evidence: Live build-your-own says no daemons, dashboards, or telemetry leaving the box.
code_truth: `kolm doctor` reaches the configured cloud health endpoint; hooks can run arbitrary telemetry commands; `kolm install` writes harness configs.
risk: The page can overstate local-only behavior for all commands.
recommended_action: Clarify which commands are local-only and which contact cloud or run user hooks.

evidence: No CI gate validates project config/schema/hook docs against runtime.
code_truth: The root suite did not include focused tests for this surface.
risk: Local policy automation can regress without detection.
recommended_action: Add schema, parser, hook execution, doctor, sidecar, and MCP config tests.
